reference.citation: Bouchaud, Farmer, Lillo (2008), How markets slowly digest changes in supply and demand, arXiv:0809.0822v1

benchmarks.context: Lillo and Farmer panel of 20 highly capitalized London Stock Exchange stocks
benchmarks.metric: Hurst exponent and corresponding autocorrelation decay exponent
benchmarks.validation_role: literature sanity benchmark; requires trade-sign data for empirical reproduction

benchmarks.context: Strategic order-splitting model compared with LSE stock averages
LSE off-book/block trade cumulative volume distribution
benchmarks.metric: Cumulative tail exponent in P(V > x) ~ x^(-alpha)
benchmarks.validation_role: tail-fit target if block trade data is acquired

benchmarks.context: Individual transaction impact for liquid LSE stocks including AZN, DGE, LLOY, SHEL, VOD
benchmarks.metric: Concave impact exponent psi in E[r | v] proportional to v^psi

benchmarks.context: Selective liquidity taking and gap-driven individual price moves in LSE studies
benchmarks.metric: Opposite-best and first-gap attribution percentages reported in paper prose
benchmarks.validation_role: requires level-2 order book data; not reproducible from bars

benchmarks.metric: R_lag amplification from lag 1 to lag 1000
Cross-sectional relation y = slope * x, y=E[vS]/E[v], x=E[vR1(v)]/E[v]

benchmarks.context: 155 most actively traded NYSE stocks in 2005
benchmarks.validation_role: spread-impact phase diagram benchmark for market with specialists

benchmarks.context: AZN traded on LSE; conditional spread decay after spread openings
benchmarks.metric: Power-law decay exponent for G(tau | Delta)
benchmarks.validation_role: spread-shock recovery target if quote data is acquired

benchmarks.context: Limit order placement distance Delta in LSE/PSE studies
Spanish Stock Exchange brokerage data hidden-order inference
benchmarks.validation_role: future hidden-order detection benchmark; requires broker/member identifiers

limitations: The arXiv source does not include the authors' raw LSE, PSE, NYSE, or Spanish Stock Exchange datasets.
These values are paper-reported benchmarks and should not be treated as regenerated reference outputs.
OHLCV bars are insufficient for most listed studies; trade, quote, order-book, and broker/member data are required depending on benchmark.
